input_features.name: delta_s
input_features.meaning: Relative longitudinal distance between target object and current NPC in Frenet s

input_features.name: delta_d
input_features.meaning: Relative lateral distance between target object and current NPC in Frenet d

input_features.name: delta_v
input_features.meaning: Relative speed between target object and current NPC

input_features.name: euclid_dist
input_features.meaning: Euclidean distance between target object and current NPC

input_features.name: d_final
input_features.meaning: Current NPC lateral offset from track centerline

input_features.name: ds_dt
input_features.meaning: Approximate longitudinal speed in Frenet coordinates

input_features.name: dd_dt
input_features.meaning: Approximate lateral speed in Frenet coordinates

input_features.name: d2d_dt2
input_features.meaning: Approximate lateral acceleration in Frenet coordinates

input_features.name: left_margin
input_features.meaning: Remaining free space to left track boundary

input_features.name: right_margin
input_features.meaning: Remaining free space to right track boundary

output_definition.name: delta_d_pred
output_definition.meaning: Predicted lateral offset increment relative to the current state over a short horizon
output_definition.unity_behavior: Use this scalar as a target lateral adjustment, then smoothly update local x_ref and y_ref

runtime_note: Unity should preprocess input using imputer_statistics, mean, and scale, run ONNX inference, read output[0] as delta_d_pred, clip if needed, then convert it into a smoothed local d_target and finally rebuild local x_ref/y_ref.
